exercises.title: Match AI feature to use case
exercises.instructions:
Match each AI capability to the HDFC/Razorpay use case:
A: Structured extraction
B: Classification with routing
C: Summarization
D: Sentiment analysis
E: Batch document processing

Use cases:
1. 50,000 salary slips → extract name, income, employer each night
2. 10,000 customer feedback messages → score happy/neutral/unhappy
3. Support ticket arrives → decide: payment team, fraud team, or tech team?
4. 200-page RBI regulation PDF → 5-bullet executive summary for branch manager
5. 1,000 contract notes from NSE → extract symbol, qty, price, tax
exercises.expected_solution:
A: Structured extraction → Use cases 1 and 5
   1: Salary slips → name, income, employer (BeanOutputConverter<SalarySlipExtract>)
   5: Contract notes → symbol, qty, price, tax (BeanOutputConverter<TradeExtract>)
   Same pattern: unstructured text → typed Java record

B: Classification with routing → Use case 3
   Support ticket → TicketCategory enum + urgency + requiresHuman
   Routing: category=FRAUD_REPORT + urgency=5 → immediately to fraud team
   Classification: AI picks one of N labels + provides metadata for routing

C: Summarization → Use case 4
   Long document → concise summary (3-5 bullets)
   System prompt: 'Summarize in exactly 5 bullets, max 20 words each'
   Good for: regulatory updates, board reports, customer statements

D: Sentiment analysis → Use case 2
   Feedback text → sentiment score + label (POSITIVE/NEUTRAL/NEGATIVE)
   Advanced: also extract the specific aspect (app speed, customer service, charges)
   Use: priority queue for negative feedback responses

E: Batch document processing → Use cases 1 and 5 (large volume)
   Same as A but emphasizing the volume/parallelism aspect
   Virtual threads + Bucket4j rate limiting for parallel AI calls
   Split into high-confidence (auto) and low-confidence (human review) queues

test_cases.expected:
A: Structured extraction → Use cases 1 and 5
   1: Salary slips → name, income, employer (BeanOutputConverter<SalarySlipExtract>)
   5: Contract notes → symbol, qty, price, tax (BeanOutputConverter<TradeExtract>)
   Same pattern: unstructured text → typed Java record

B: Classification with routing → Use case 3
   Support ticket → TicketCategory enum + urgency + requiresHuman
   Routing: category=FRAUD_REPORT + urgency=5 → immediately to fraud team
   Classification: AI picks one of N labels + provides metadata for routing

C: Summarization → Use case 4
   Long document → concise summary (3-5 bullets)
   System prompt: 'Summarize in exactly 5 bullets, max 20 words each'
   Good for: regulatory updates, board reports, customer statements

D: Sentiment analysis → Use case 2
   Feedback text → sentiment score + label (POSITIVE/NEUTRAL/NEGATIVE)
   Advanced: also extract the specific aspect (app speed, customer service, charges)
   Use: priority queue for negative feedback responses

E: Batch document processing → Use cases 1 and 5 (large volume)
   Same as A but emphasizing the volume/parallelism aspect
   Virtual threads + Bucket4j rate limiting for parallel AI calls
   Split into high-confidence (auto) and low-confidence (human review) queues

hints: Extraction: unstructured → typed record
Classification: text → one of N labels + metadata
Summarization: long → short structured summary

exercises.title: Complete the structured extraction setup
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks for salary slip extraction.

blanks.hint: the record type to convert to
pass the Class object to the converter
generates the JSON schema for the AI
parses the AI's JSON string into the record

hints: BeanOutputConverter<T>: T is the record type
getFormat(): JSON schema the AI uses as output specification
convert(jsonString): parses AI response → Java record

exercises.title: Support ticket classifier
exercises.instructions:
Write SupportTicketClassifier:
1. TicketClassification record: category (enum), urgency (1-5), requiresHuman
2. TicketCategory enum: PAYMENT_FAILURE, REFUND, TECHNICAL, FRAUD, GENERAL
3. classify(ticketText): BeanOutputConverter → structured result
4. @SpringBootTest: converter schema contains 'urgency'
hints: requiresHuman: urgency>=4 → human agent; AI makes this decision based on prompt rules
urgency=5 for FRAUD: ensures fraud tickets go to human immediately
BeanOutputConverter: enum fields must be Java enums — AI returns the enum name as string

exercises.title: Salary slip extraction with confidence gate
exercises.instructions:
Write SalarySlipExtractor with confidence gating:
1. SalarySlipExtract record: name, employer, monthlyGrossIncomePaise, confidence
2. extract(text): returns full extract
3. extractSafe(text): returns Optional.empty() if confidence < 70
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): high confidence → present, low confidence → empty
hints: confidence >= 70: high confidence threshold (tune based on domain)
Optional.empty(): routes to human review queue without crashing
Monthly gross in paise: ₹85,000 = 8,500,000 paise

exercises.title: AI summarization service
exercises.instructions:
Write DocumentSummarizer:
1. System prompt: 'Summarize in exactly 3 bullet points, max 25 words each'
2. SummaryResult record: bullets (List<String>), wordCount, documentTitle
3. summarize(title, fullText): returns SummaryResult
4. @SpringBootTest: schema has 'bullets' field
hints: Exactly 3 bullets: enforce in system prompt — AI follows constraints reliably
truncate to 8000 chars: avoid token limit errors on very long documents
wordCount: include in prompt so AI can calibrate summary depth

exercises.title: Customer feedback sentiment analysis
exercises.instructions:
Write FeedbackSentimentAnalyzer:
1. SentimentResult: sentiment (POSITIVE/NEUTRAL/NEGATIVE), score (-1.0 to 1.0), aspects (List<String>)
2. analyze(feedbackText): AI scores + identifies aspects (app, charges, speed)
3. Prioritize: score < -0.5 → urgent negative feedback
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): schema valid, urgency logic correct
hints: Score -1.0 to 1.0: granular (not just 3 buckets)
aspects: list of what the feedback covers (multiple allowed)
isUrgent(): computed from score — don't store a separate 'isUrgent' boolean separately

exercises.title: Batch document processing with virtual threads
exercises.instructions:
Write BatchDocumentProcessor:
1. Process list of salary slip texts in parallel using virtual threads
2. Bucket4j rate limit: max 20 concurrent AI calls
3. Split results: highConfidence (>=70) and reviewQueue (<70)
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): batch count, split correctness
hints: Virtual threads: newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor() — lightweight, one per document
CopyOnWriteArrayList: thread-safe for parallel writes from virtual threads
f.get(30, TimeUnit.SECONDS): timeout per document (avoid hanging on slow AI)

exercises.title: Trade contract note extractor for Zerodha
exercises.instructions:
Write TradeContractExtractor for Zerodha:
1. TradeExtract record: symbol, quantity, price (paise), tradeType (BUY/SELL), brokerage (paise), tradeDate, confidence
2. extract(contractNoteText): structured extraction
3. totalValue(): quantity × price
4. @SpringBootTest: record computes totalValue correctly
hints: totalValuePaise = qty × pricePaise: integer arithmetic, no floating point
confidence >= 75: slightly higher threshold for financial data (more critical than HR docs)

exercises.title: AI fallback on error
exercises.instructions:
Write AIServiceWithFallback:
1. Primary: call AI for document classification
2. Fallback: if AI throws exception, use rule-based classifier
3. RuleBasedClassifier: keyword matching (fast, always available)
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): primary works, fallback activates on AI error
hints: ALWAYS have a fallback: AI APIs have downtime, rate limits, errors
Rule-based fallback: keyword matching — not as smart, but 100% reliable
Log fallback activation: monitor how often AI fails (alert if > 5%)

exercises.title: Extraction quality analysis
exercises.instructions:
HDFC's salary slip extractor ran on 1,000 documents. Analyze these results:

```
Extraction Results:
  High confidence (>=70): 920 documents (92%)
  Low confidence (<70):   80 documents (8%)   → sent to human review
  Extraction errors:      15 documents         → exception thrown
  Manual review (human):  95 documents         → 80 low + 15 errors

Accuracy check on 50 random high-confidence docs:
  Correct extractions:  45/50 (90%)
  Wrong income amount:  3/50 (6%)
  Wrong employer name:  2/50 (4%)
```

1. Overall accuracy estimate for the full pipeline?
2. Is 90% accuracy on high-confidence extractions good enough for loan processing?
3. What's wrong with the extraction for 3 wrong income amounts?
4. How would you improve?
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Overall accuracy estimate:
   920 high-confidence docs × 90% accuracy = 828 correctly extracted
   80 low-confidence → human review (100% accurate by humans)
   15 errors → human review (100% accurate)
   Total correct: 828 (AI) + 95 (human) = 923 / 1000 = 92.3% overall pipeline accuracy
   Remaining wrong: 92 documents (920 × 10% AI errors) — some have incorrect data in DB!
   This 7.7% wrong rate (from high-confidence incorrect extractions) is a risk.

2. Is 90% good enough for loan processing?
   NO — financial decisions on incorrect income data = loan defaults, fraud, regulatory fines
   Acceptable: 99%+ for financial extraction (not 90%)
   3 wrong incomes per 50 = 60 wrong per 1,000 = 60 loans with incorrect income data
   HDFC does 100,000 loans/month → 6,000 with wrong income → serious compliance issue
   Fix: lower confidence threshold to 80 (more sent to human review until accuracy improves)

3. What's wrong with income extraction:
   Possible issues:
   a) Gross vs net confusion: salary slip shows both; AI picks wrong one
   b) Monthly vs annual: slip says '₹10,20,000 per annum' → should be ₹85,000/month
   c) Variable pay: basic + HRA + allowances + bonus → AI may sum wrong components
   d) Different document formats: some companies format slips differently
   Root cause: prompt doesn't specify 'MONTHLY GROSS excluding variables and bonuses'

4. Improvements:
   a) Prompt fix: 'Extract monthly GROSS fixed salary only. Exclude variable pay, bonuses, HRA if separate.'
   b) Validation: extracted_income / 100 must be in range ₹10,000 to ₹10,00,000 (sanity check)
   c) Extract multiple fields: base, HRA, allowances, total → compare with stated total
   d) Lower threshold to 80% temporarily → more to human review → catch errors
   e) Run on test set of 100 known documents → measure accuracy per document format

test_cases.expected:
1. Overall accuracy estimate:
   920 high-confidence docs × 90% accuracy = 828 correctly extracted
   80 low-confidence → human review (100% accurate by humans)
   15 errors → human review (100% accurate)
   Total correct: 828 (AI) + 95 (human) = 923 / 1000 = 92.3% overall pipeline accuracy
   Remaining wrong: 92 documents (920 × 10% AI errors) — some have incorrect data in DB!
   This 7.7% wrong rate (from high-confidence incorrect extractions) is a risk.

2. Is 90% good enough for loan processing?
   NO — financial decisions on incorrect income data = loan defaults, fraud, regulatory fines
   Acceptable: 99%+ for financial extraction (not 90%)
   3 wrong incomes per 50 = 60 wrong per 1,000 = 60 loans with incorrect income data
   HDFC does 100,000 loans/month → 6,000 with wrong income → serious compliance issue
   Fix: lower confidence threshold to 80 (more sent to human review until accuracy improves)

3. What's wrong with income extraction:
   Possible issues:
   a) Gross vs net confusion: salary slip shows both; AI picks wrong one
   b) Monthly vs annual: slip says '₹10,20,000 per annum' → should be ₹85,000/month
   c) Variable pay: basic + HRA + allowances + bonus → AI may sum wrong components
   d) Different document formats: some companies format slips differently
   Root cause: prompt doesn't specify 'MONTHLY GROSS excluding variables and bonuses'

4. Improvements:
   a) Prompt fix: 'Extract monthly GROSS fixed salary only. Exclude variable pay, bonuses, HRA if separate.'
   b) Validation: extracted_income / 100 must be in range ₹10,000 to ₹10,00,000 (sanity check)
   c) Extract multiple fields: base, HRA, allowances, total → compare with stated total
   d) Lower threshold to 80% temporarily → more to human review → catch errors
   e) Run on test set of 100 known documents → measure accuracy per document format

hints: 90% for loans = 6,000 wrong/month: unacceptable for financial decisions
Gross vs net: most common extraction error for salary documents
Validation range: sanity check catches impossible values (income > ₹10 crore → flag)

exercises.title: Complete HDFC loan document processing pipeline
exercises.instructions:
Build complete pipeline:
1. LoanDocument: salarySlip + bankStatement + idProof texts
2. LoanApplicationExtract: name, income, employer, panNumber, bankBalance
3. LoanEligibilityResult: eligible, reason, maxLoanAmount
4. LoanProcessor: extract → validate → check eligibility
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): extraction record, validation logic, eligibility check
hints: maxLoan = 60 × monthly income: industry-standard EMI-to-income calculation
isValid(): null checks + range validation — reject garbage before eligibility check

exercises.title: AI-powered search in REST API
exercises.instructions:
Add semantic search to Zerodha's stock research API:
1. StockResearchEntry: symbol, sector, description, lastUpdated
2. Embed descriptions in InMemoryVectorStore on startup
3. GET /api/stocks/search?q=renewable energy: semantic search → top-3 stocks
4. @WebMvcTest: search endpoint returns results
hints: Semantic search in REST: user types natural language → finds semantically matching stocks
'solar power' → finds 'renewable energy' stocks (no keyword match needed)
@MockBean StockSemanticSearchService: avoid real vector search in WebMvcTest

exercises.title: Complete HDFC document intelligence service
exercises.instructions:
Build complete document intelligence:
1. DocumentClassifier: classifies doc type (SALARY_SLIP, BANK_STATEMENT, ID_PROOF, INVOICE)
2. DocumentExtractor: routes to correct extraction strategy per type
3. ExtractionResult: docType, extractedFields (Map<String,String>), confidence
4. ValidationService: checks required fields per doc type
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): classify, extract, validate, reject-low-confidence
hints: DocType routing: classify first → extract with type-specific strategy
REQUIRED map: different doc types need different fields
Validation gates: both confidence AND required fields must pass

exercises.title: AI summarization pipeline for Zerodha weekly digest
exercises.instructions:
Build Zerodha weekly market digest generator:
1. MarketEvent: symbol, eventType, description, date
2. WeeklyDigest record: topStories (3 bullets), marketSentiment, riskAlert
3. DigestGenerator: takes List<MarketEvent>, generates digest via AI
4. Format: investor-friendly, no jargon, Hindi-friendly names for companies
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): digest schema, events processed
hints: eventsAnalyzed: set explicitly after AI call (AI may not count correctly)
Exactly 3 bullets: enforce in system prompt — AI follows count constraints reliably
Common names: 'Reliance' not 'RELIANCE INDUSTRIES LIMITED' — investor-friendly

exercises.title: Complete M26 T3 AI Features synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M26 T3 synthesis:
1. DocClassifier: classify SALARY_SLIP/ID_PROOF/UNKNOWN
2. StructuredExtractor: BeanOutputConverter for SalarySlipExtract
3. ConfidenceGate: extractSafe() → Optional
4. TicketClassifier: classify ticket → urgency + requiresHuman
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): classify, extract schema, confidence gate, ticket urgency
6. All 4 pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M26 T3 AI Features synthesis
hints: BeanOutputConverter.getFormat(): schema contains field names
Optional.empty(): low confidence routes to human review
urgency=5 for fraud: highest urgency → always requires human
